name: Kermit mk III
description: 4-channel tempo-synced wavetable modulator. Each channel selects from LFO/Oscillator/Envelope/Random/S&H types, each with three modes. Internal modulation matrix, 8 morphing presets, microSD wavetable import.

controls.name: Frequency
controls.description: Coarse frequency of channels A and B. Acts as tempo divider in tempo-synced modes, V/oct-scaled in Oscillator modes. Holding the channel button accesses Fine Tune.
controls.detail: LFO: rate · Osc: pitch · Env/Random: rate/division

controls.name: Amplitude
controls.description: Output level for channels A and B. Attenuates toward zero in bipolar bias modes. Hold channel button to access Phase parameter.
controls.detail: attenuator · 12Vpp bipolar, 0-6V unipolar, or 1Vpp video mode

controls.name: Waveform
controls.description: Selects wave within the active bank and morphs between adjacent waves. Hold channel button to jump between wave banks.

controls.name: A / B buttons
controls.description: Hotkey buttons. Hold + turn other knobs to access deep parameters (Fine Tune, Phase, Bank). Hold while turning a destination knob on another channel to assign A or B as cross-modulation source.
controls.detail: hotkey modifier · source for mod matrix

controls.name: C knob
controls.description: Single knob for channel C. Default: coarse frequency. Other parameters accessed via button-combo hotkeys (hold combinations to scan Amplitude, Waveform, Fine Tune, Phase, Bank).

controls.name: D knob
controls.description: Single knob for channel D. Same hotkey scheme as C knob but requires the Tap button as an extra modifier to reach D parameters.

controls.name: C/D button
controls.description: Shared channel button for C and D. Hold + other buttons to reach C or D parameter slots. Scrolls past C into D options in the channel menu.

controls.name: Encoder (push)
controls.description: Rotary encoder with click. Turns to navigate menus and change values. Holding and turning a knob assigns that channel's CV input to that parameter as an attenuverter.

controls.name: Preset / Edit button
controls.description: Enables the 8-slot preset manager. Press again for Morph mode (smooth CV-driven interpolation). Double-click encoder while preset is active to enter edit-save mode.

controls.name: Tap button
controls.description: Master tap-tempo button. Tap rhythmically to set global BPM. Hold encoder + press Tap to re-sync all tempo-aware channels on the next event.

controls.name: Preset CV attenuator
controls.description: Attenuates or offsets the Preset CV input — behavior set by CTL menu (CV+Offset, CV+Atten, Trig+Offset, Trig Random).
controls.detail: role set by global CTL option

inputs.name: 1V/Oct (CH A, B)
inputs.description: Per-channel 1V/oct input in Oscillator modes. In LFO Reset or Tempo mode acts as sync trigger. In Envelope/S&H modes acts as gate/trigger input.
inputs.voltage: 1V/oct in Osc, trigger in other modes

inputs.name: CV (CH A, B)
inputs.description: Per-channel assignable modulation input. Hold encoder + turn destination knob to route CV to Frequency, Amplitude, or Waveform with attenuverter amount.

inputs.name: CV (CH C, D)
inputs.description: Shared CV input per channel. Assignable via hotkey combos. In Envelope/S&H modes doubles as trigger/voltage-to-sample input.

inputs.name: Tap
inputs.description: External clock input for global tempo. Feeds into the tempo-aware modes across all four channels.

inputs.name: Preset CV
inputs.description: Scans through the 8 preset slots. Behavior set by CTL option: direct CV scan, trig-advance, or trig-random.

outputs.name: A
outputs.description: Main output for channel A. Voltage range set by Voltage Range menu (12Vpp bipolar, 0-6V unipolar, or 1Vpp video).

outputs.name: B
outputs.description: Main output for channel B. Voltage range per channel-B menu setting.

behaviors.name: LFO modes
behaviors.description: Three LFO modes: Free (runs freely, 1V/oct on input), Reset (resets to Phase on trigger at 1V/oct jack), Tap Tempo (rate is division/multiplication of master BPM; 1V/oct jack resets on next tempo tick). Wavetable shape selectable across 512 waves.

behaviors.name: Oscillator modes
behaviors.description: Three audio-rate modes: Oscillator (tracks 1V/oct, character slightly fuzzy), Unison (detuned internal second voice for thicker tone), Code Scan (reads the module's own program code as waveform data — harsh, glitchy). 16 Hz to ~1 kHz useful range.

behaviors.name: Envelope modes
behaviors.trigger: channel Type → Envelope · trigger → 1V/oct (A/B) or CV (C/D) jack
behaviors.description: Three modes: 1-shot (plays selected wave once per trigger, retriggers freely), Gated Repeat (cycles while gate is high), Tempo Envelope (auto-fires at master-clock divisions). Factory wave banks 6-7 are unipolar shapes intended for 0-6V bias Envelope use.

behaviors.name: Random modes
behaviors.description: Three flavors: Smooth Random (fluctuating voltages at frequency-controlled rate), Stepped Random (new value each cycle), Tempo Random (stepped values at master-clock divisions). Waveform control has no effect in random modes.

behaviors.name: Sample & Hold modes (A/B only)
behaviors.trigger: channel Type → S+H · channel is A or B
behaviors.description: Trigger into 1V/oct, voltage to sample into CV input. Three modes: Sample+Hold (snapshot on trig), Track+Hold (pass while gate high, hold when low), Tempo S+H (samples at clock divisions). CV path is not calibrated — artifacts are part of the character. Not available on C/D.

behaviors.name: Osc Copy (C/D only)
behaviors.trigger: channel Type → Osc Copy on C or D
behaviors.description: Channels C or D copy all parameters except Phase from any other channel. Lets you set up quadrature LFOs, offset phases, or detuned unisons without duplicating modulation assignments. Unique to C/D.

behaviors.name: Internal modulation matrix
behaviors.trigger: hold source channel button + turn destination knob
behaviors.description: Any channel output can modulate Frequency, Amplitude, or Waveform of any other channel. Assignment is an attenuverter — center=zero, CCW inverts, CW adds. Enables cross-modulation, self-patching-free complex modulation, and AM/FM between channels. Hold encoder on main screen to see all active assignments.

behaviors.name: CV input assignment
behaviors.description: The per-channel CV input is not hardwired to a parameter. Instead it's routed as an attenuverter to whatever parameter you assign. Holding encoder + turning Freq, Amp, or Waveform assigns the CV to that destination. Clear via menu or return the knob to center.

behaviors.name: Preset morph via CV
behaviors.trigger: Preset button pressed twice (Morph mode active)
behaviors.description: In Morph mode all managed controls lock; the Preset CV input smoothly crossfades between stored slots. Use the encoder to pick the base preset (sets menu/mode settings that can't morph). Apply CV to interpolate analog parameters across 8 snapshots. Ideal for live performance scenes.

behaviors.name: Character menu
behaviors.description: Per-channel distortion flavor: Clean (16-bit), 12bit, 8bit, NoMorph (hard wave transitions), Depraved (8-bit + 32-sample resolution, SCC chip-like). Most audible in Oscillator modes. NoMorph forces glitchy stepped waveform selection instead of smooth interpolation.

behaviors.name: Voltage range bias
behaviors.description: Three bias modes: 12Vpp (±6V bipolar, default for LFO/Osc/Random), 0-6V (unipolar — recommended for Envelope mode so output rests at 0V between hits), 1Vpp (attenuated, intended for video synth modules). Changing bias affects the relationship between amplitude and envelope rest voltage.

behaviors.name: microSD wavetable loading
behaviors.trigger: insert SD with 1.wav · Global menu → Load Waves from SD
behaviors.description: Accepts one 1-channel 16-bit WAV containing 64 waveforms × 256 samples (32KB total). Organized internally as 8 banks × 8 waves. WaveEdit from Synthesis Technology recommended for authoring. Module reboots after load; presets survive firmware updates.

patchIdeas.name: Quadrature LFOs from one source
patchIdeas.patch: CH A: Free LFO, sine wave · CH C: Osc Copy A · Hold C + turn encoder to set Phase +90° · CH D: Osc Copy A, Phase 180° · three synced phase-offset LFOs for quadrature panning or phase-modulated swarms without patch cables
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* CH A: LFO Free, sine
* CH C: Osc Copy A, Phase 90°
* CH D: Osc Copy A, Phase 180°

VCA L:
VCA R:

- Kermit mk III (A) >> VCA L (CV)
- Kermit mk III (C) >> VCA R (CV)
- Kermit mk III (D) >> Filter (Cutoff) // phase-offset vibrato

patchIdeas.name: Clocked envelope bank
patchIdeas.patch: Global tap tempo synced to master clock · CH A: Tempo Envelope, bank 6 shapes, ÷8 · CH B: Tempo Envelope ÷4 · CH C: Tempo Envelope ÷2 · three rhythmic envelopes at related divisions feed VCAs across a drum bus · morph wave for different envelope shapes
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* CH A: Tempo Env ÷8, bank 6
* CH B: Tempo Env ÷4, bank 6
* CH C: Tempo Env ÷2, bank 6

Clock:
VCA 1:
VCA 2:
VCA 3:

- Clock (Out) c> Kermit mk III (Tap)
- Kermit mk III (A) >> VCA 1 (CV)
- Kermit mk III (B) >> VCA 2 (CV)
- Kermit mk III (C) >> VCA 3 (CV)

patchIdeas.name: FM pair with cross-modulation
patchIdeas.patch: CH A: Oscillator mode, pitched by sequencer · CH B: Oscillator mode, Unison · hold A button + turn B Frequency knob (center → CW) to route A output as FM into B · hold B button + turn A Waveform to route B as wavetable mod on A · entwined dual VCO with no cables · C-D free for further modulation
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* CH A: Oscillator
* CH B: Oscillator Unison
* Mod matrix: A→B Freq, B→A Waveform

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Kermit mk III (1V/Oct A)
- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Kermit mk III (1V/Oct B)
- Kermit mk III (A) -> Mixer (In 1)
- Kermit mk III (B) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: Tempo-synced random voltages
patchIdeas.patch: CH A: Tempo Random ÷16 (chord CV) · CH B: Tempo Random ÷4 (filter CV) · CH C: Stepped Random free-running (velocity) · CH D: Smooth Random (slow pad modulation) · quantizer downstream on A · four rhythmic random sources from one module — replaces a Turing plus Pam's plus S&H
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* CH A: Tempo Random ÷16
* CH B: Tempo Random ÷4
* CH C: Stepped Random
* CH D: Smooth Random (slow)

Clock:
Quantizer:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:

- Clock (Out) c> Kermit mk III (Tap)
- Kermit mk III (A) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Kermit mk III (B) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Kermit mk III (C) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Preset morph performance scene
patchIdeas.patch: Configure 8 preset slots from sparse → dense modulation · Preset scope: all params · CTL: CV+Offset · slow ramp (Maths cycle) → Preset CV · press Preset button twice (Morph) · complete modulation scene evolves across 8 stored states · single CV drives a live performance progression
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* 8 presets stored (sparse to dense)
* Preset mode: Morph · CTL: CV+Offset

MATHS:
* CH 1: slow ramp, cycle ON

- MATHS.CH 1 (OUT) >> Kermit mk III (Preset CV)
- Kermit mk III (A) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Kermit mk III (B) >> VCA (CV)
- Kermit mk III (C) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Kermit mk III (D) >> FX (Time)

patchIdeas.name: Gated-repeat burst generator
patchIdeas.patch: CH A: Gated Repeat envelope, bank 6, rate at audio-rate range · gate source → 1V/oct A · length of gate = length of burst · CH A output → drum module accent CV · generates rapid envelope bursts only while gate is high · classic jungle snare roll generator
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* CH A: Gated Repeat Env, bank 6, fast rate
* CH A Voltage Range: 0-6V

Gate Seq:
Drum:

- Gate Seq (Out) g> Kermit mk III (1V/Oct A) // gate length = burst length
- Kermit mk III (A) >> Drum (Accent CV)
- Drum (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Self-modulating complex LFO
patchIdeas.patch: CH A: slow Free LFO sine · hold A + turn B Frequency (mod amount 50%) → A modulates B's rate · CH B: medium Tap Tempo LFO, saw · hold B + turn A Waveform → B modulates A's wavetable position · feedback complex motion between two channels — single CV out that never repeats
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* CH A: Free LFO sine, slow
* CH B: Tap LFO saw, medium
* Mod matrix: A→B Freq (50%), B→A Waveform (40%)

Filter:

- Kermit mk III (A) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Kermit mk III (B) >> Filter (Res)

patchIdeas.name: Code Scan noise voice
patchIdeas.patch: CH A: Oscillator mode Code Scan · Character: Depraved · pitched by sequencer · audible as harsh digital noise that changes timbre with Waveform knob (scans program memory) · CH B: Envelope for amplitude · one channel noise source, one channel VCA envelope, from one module
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* CH A: Osc Code Scan, Character Depraved
* CH B: 1-shot Env, bank 6

Sequencer:
VCA:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Kermit mk III (1V/Oct A)
- Sequencer (Gate) t> Kermit mk III (1V/Oct B)
- Kermit mk III (A) -> VCA (In)
- Kermit mk III (B) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Mixer (In)

patchIdeas.name: Four-voice chord sample & hold
patchIdeas.patch: CH A: S+H, trigger on 1V/oct A, noise source → CV A · CH B: Osc Copy A, phase 90° so it samples on A's B-equivalent · CH C/D: Random/Random tempo · four quantized voices out feed four VCOs for polyphonic chord sequencing · gate from clock drives all
patchIdeas.diagram:
Kermit mk III:
* CH A: S+H
* CH B: S+H (offset trigger)
* CH C: Tempo Random ÷4
* CH D: Tempo Random ÷2

Clock:
Noise:
Quantizer:
VCO 1:
VCO 2:

- Clock (Out) t> Kermit mk III (1V/Oct A)
- Noise (Out) >> Kermit mk III (CV A)
- Kermit mk III (A) p> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO 1 (V/Oct)
- Kermit mk III (C) p> VCO 2 (V/Oct)
